# solution.py
"""TeamSync / Team Collaboration Manager.

A self-contained, thread-safe in-memory platform for coordinated team
management.  The public surface is the :class:`TeamCollaborationManager`
which orchestrates three cooperating modules built in dependency order:

1. **User management** -- the foundation every other feature relies on.
2. **Scheduling & communication** -- projects, tasks and messaging, which
   depend on user profiles existing first.
3. **Performance tracking & reporting** -- dashboards and reports derived
   from the user and task data produced by the modules above.

Standard library only.  All mutating and reading operations share a single
re-entrant lock so the manager is safe to use from many threads at once.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import copy
import csv
import io
import threading
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime
from enum import Enum
from typing import Callable, Dict, List, Optional, Tuple


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Enumerations
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TaskStatus(Enum):
    """Lifecycle states a task can occupy."""

    NOT_STARTED = "not_started"
    IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"
    COMPLETED = "completed"


class UserRole(Enum):
    """Authorization roles a user can hold."""

    MEMBER = "member"
    ADMIN = "admin"


class ReportFormat(Enum):
    """Supported export formats for generated reports."""

    CSV = "csv"
    PDF = "pdf"


class ReportType(Enum):
    """Categories of report the manager can produce."""

    PROJECT_PROGRESS = "project_progress"
    TEAM_PERFORMANCE = "team_performance"
    INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE = "individual_performance"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Exception hierarchy
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationError(Exception):
    """Base class for every error raised by the manager."""


class ValidationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when supplied input fails validation."""


class NotFoundError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a referenced entity does not exist."""


class AuthorizationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an actor is not permitted to perform an action."""


class DuplicateError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a uniqueness constraint would be violated."""


class ConflictError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an operation conflicts with current state."""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain entities
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@dataclass
class Attachment:
    """A file attached to a message."""

    filename: str
    size: int
    uploaded_by: int
    uploaded_at: datetime


@dataclass
class User:
    """A team member profile."""

    id: int
    username: str
    email: str
    role: UserRole
    created_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Project:
    """A scheduling container that owns tasks and messages."""

    id: int
    name: str
    description: str
    start_date: datetime
    end_date: datetime
    owner_id: int


@dataclass
class Task:
    """A unit of work that can be scheduled, assigned and tracked."""

    id: int
    project_id: int
    title: str
    status: TaskStatus = TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED
    assignee_id: Optional[int] = None
    deadline: Optional[datetime] = None
    started_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    completed_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Message:
    """A real-time communication item bound to a project or a task."""

    id: int
    author_id: int
    body: str
    project_id: Optional[int] = None
    task_id: Optional[int] = None
    parent_id: Optional[int] = None
    attachments: List[Attachment] = field(default_factory=list)
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    edited_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Rating:
    """A peer performance rating."""

    id: int
    rater_id: int
    ratee_id: int
    score: int
    feedback: str = ""
    task_id: Optional[int] = None
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Repository -- a thin, lock-sharing container for one entity type
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _Repository:
    """A simple id-keyed store that shares the manager's lock.

    The store deliberately exposes ``_lock`` so callers can confirm that a
    single lock governs every module, guaranteeing consistent concurrent
    access across the whole manager.
    """

    def __init__(self, lock: threading.RLock) -> None:
        self._lock = lock
        self._items: Dict[int, object] = {}


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Manager
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationManager:
    """Coordinates users, projects, tasks, messaging and reporting."""

    def __init__(self, now: Optional[Callable[[], datetime]] = None) -> None:
        self._now: Callable[[], datetime] = now if now is not None else datetime.now
        self._lock = threading.RLock()
        self._id_counter = 0

        # Each module shares the single manager lock.
        self.users = _Repository(self._lock)
        self.projects = _Repository(self._lock)
        self.tasks = _Repository(self._lock)
        self.messages = _Repository(self._lock)
        self.performance = _Repository(self._lock)
        self.reports = _Repository(self._lock)

    # -- internal helpers ---------------------------------------------------
    def _next_id(self) -> int:
        self._id_counter += 1
        return self._id_counter

    def _clock(self) -> datetime:
        return self._now()

    @staticmethod
    def _clone(obj):
        return copy.deepcopy(obj)

    def _require_user(self, user_id: int) -> User:
        user = self.users._items.get(user_id)
        if user is None:
            raise NotFoundError("User {0} not found".format(user_id))
        return user  # type: ignore[return-value]

    def _require_project(self, project_id: int) -> Project:
        project = self.projects._items.get(project_id)
        if project is None:
            raise NotFoundError("Project {0} not found".format(project_id))
        return project  # type: ignore[return-value]

    def _require_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        task = self.tasks._items.get(task_id)
        if task is None:
            raise NotFoundError("Task {0} not found".format(task_id))
        return task  # type: ignore[return-value]

    def _require_message(self, message_id: int) -> Message:
        message = self.messages._items.get(message_id)
        if message is None:
            raise NotFoundError("Message {0} not found".format(message_id))
        return message  # type: ignore[return-value]

    def _tasks_for_assignee(self, user_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        return [
            t
            for t in self.tasks._items.values()
            if t.assignee_id == user_id  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
        ]

    @staticmethod
    def _validate_email(email: str) -> str:
        if email is None:
            raise ValidationError("Email is required")
        cleaned = email.strip()
        if not cleaned or "@" not in cleaned:
            raise ValidationError("A valid email address is required")
        return cleaned

    @staticmethod
    def _validate_name(value: str, label: str) -> str:
        if value is None or not value.strip():
            raise ValidationError("{0} must not be empty".format(label))
        return value.strip()

    # ==================================================================
    # 1. User management module
    # ==================================================================
    def register_user(
        self,
        username: str,
        email: str,
        role: UserRole = UserRole.MEMBER,
    ) -> User:
        with self._lock:
            clean_username = self._validate_name(username, "Username")
            clean_email = self._validate_email(email)
            if not isinstance(role, UserRole):
                raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")
            for existing in self.users._items.values():
                if existing.username == clean_username:  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
                    raise DuplicateError("Username already in use")
                if existing.email == clean_email:  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
                    raise DuplicateError("Email already in use")
            user = User(
                id=self._next_id(),
                username=clean_username,
                email=clean_email,
                role=role,
                created_at=self._clock(),
            )
            self.users._items[user.id] = user
            return self._clone(user)

    def get_user(self, user_id: int) -> User:
        with self._lock:
            return self._clone(self._require_user(user_id))

    def list_users(self) -> List[User]:
        with self._lock:
            return [
                self._clone(u)
                for u in sorted(self.users._items.values(), key=lambda x: x.id)
            ]

    def update_user(
        self,
        user_id: int,
        username: Optional[str] = None,
        email: Optional[str] = None,
        role: Optional[UserRole] = None,
    ) -> User:
        with self._lock:
            user = self._require_user(user_id)
            if username is not None:
                clean_username = self._validate_name(username, "Username")
                for existing in self.users._items.values():
                    if (
                        existing.id != user_id
                        and existing.username == clean_username  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
                    ):
                        raise DuplicateError("Username already in use")
                user.username = clean_username
            if email is not None:
                clean_email = self._validate_email(email)
                for existing in self.users._items.values():
                    if (
                        existing.id != user_id
                        and existing.email == clean_email  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
                    ):
                        raise DuplicateError("Email already in use")
                user.email = clean_email
            if role is not None:
                if not isinstance(role, UserRole):
                    raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")
                user.role = role
            return self._clone(user)

    def delete_user(self, user_id: int) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            for project in self.projects._items.values():
                if project.owner_id == user_id:  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
                    raise ConflictError("User still owns a project")
            for task in self.tasks._items.values():
                if (
                    task.assignee_id == user_id  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
                    and task.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
                ):
                    raise ConflictError("User has open assigned tasks")
            del self.users._items[user_id]

    # ==================================================================
    # 2. Scheduling module -- projects
    # ==================================================================
    def create_project(
        self,
        name: str,
        start_date: datetime,
        end_date: datetime,
        owner_id: int,
        description: str = "",
    ) -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            clean_name = self._validate_name(name, "Project name")
            if end_date < start_date:
                raise ValidationError("end_date must not be before start_date")
            self._require_user(owner_id)
            project = Project(
                id=self._next_id(),
                name=clean_name,
                description=description or "",
                start_date=start_date,
                end_date=end_date,
                owner_id=owner_id,
            )
            self.projects._items[project.id] = project
            return self._clone(project)

    def get_project(self, project_id: int) -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            return self._clone(self._require_project(project_id))

    def list_projects(self) -> List[Project]:
        with self._lock:
            return [
                self._clone(p)
                for p in sorted(self.projects._items.values(), key=lambda x: x.id)
            ]

    def update_project(
        self,
        project_id: int,
        name: Optional[str] = None,
        start_date: Optional[datetime] = None,
        end_date: Optional[datetime] = None,
        description: Optional[str] = None,
    ) -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            project = self._require_project(project_id)
            new_name = project.name
            if name is not None:
                new_name = self._validate_name(name, "Project name")
            new_start = project.start_date if start_date is None else start_date
            new_end = project.end_date if end_date is None else end_date
            if new_end < new_start:
                raise ValidationError("end_date must not be before start_date")
            project.name = new_name
            project.start_date = new_start
            project.end_date = new_end
            if description is not None:
                project.description = description
            return self._clone(project)

    def delete_project(self, project_id: int) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            task_ids = [
                t.id
                for t in self.tasks._items.values()
                if t.project_id == project_id  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
            ]
            for task_id in task_ids:
                del self.tasks._items[task_id]
            task_id_set = set(task_ids)
            message_ids = [
                m.id
                for m in self.messages._items.values()
                if m.project_id == project_id  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
                or m.task_id in task_id_set  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
            ]
            for message_id in message_ids:
                del self.messages._items[message_id]
            del self.projects._items[project_id]

    # ==================================================================
    # 2. Scheduling module -- tasks
    # ==================================================================
    def create_task(
        self,
        project_id: int,
        title: str,
        assignee_id: Optional[int] = None,
        deadline: Optional[datetime] = None,
    ) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            project = self._require_project(project_id)
            clean_title = self._validate_name(title, "Task title")
            if assignee_id is not None:
                self._require_user(assignee_id)
            if deadline is not None and deadline < project.start_date:
                raise ValidationError("deadline must not be before project start")
            task = Task(
                id=self._next_id(),
                project_id=project_id,
                title=clean_title,
                status=TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED,
                assignee_id=assignee_id,
                deadline=deadline,
                created_at=self._clock(),
            )
            self.tasks._items[task.id] = task
            return self._clone(task)

    def get_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            return self._clone(self._require_task(task_id))

    def list_tasks_by_project(self, project_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            tasks = [
                t
                for t in self.tasks._items.values()
                if t.project_id == project_id  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
            ]
            return [
                self._clone(t) for t in sorted(tasks, key=lambda x: x.id)
            ]

    def list_tasks_by_assignee(self, assignee_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(assignee_id)
            tasks = self._tasks_for_assignee(assignee_id)
            return [
                self._clone(t) for t in sorted(tasks, key=lambda x: x.id)
            ]

    def assign_task(self, task_id: int, assignee_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            self._require_user(assignee_id)
            task.assignee_id = assignee_id
            return self._clone(task)

    def reassign_task(self, task_id: int, new_assignee_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            self._require_user(new_assignee_id)
            task.assignee_id = new_assignee_id
            return self._clone(task)

    def unassign_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            task.assignee_id = None
            return self._clone(task)

    def set_deadline(
        self, task_id: int, deadline: Optional[datetime]
    ) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            if deadline is not None:
                project = self._require_project(task.project_id)
                if deadline < project.start_date:
                    raise ValidationError(
                        "deadline must not be before project start"
                    )
            task.deadline = deadline
            return self._clone(task)

    def update_status(self, task_id: int, status: TaskStatus) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            if not isinstance(status, TaskStatus):
                raise ValidationError("status must be a TaskStatus")
            now = self._clock()
            if status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = None
            elif status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = now
            elif status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED:
                task.started_at = None
                task.completed_at = None
            task.status = status
            return self._clone(task)

    # ==================================================================
    # 2. Communication module -- messaging
    # ==================================================================
    def post_message(
        self,
        author_id: int,
        body: str,
        project_id: Optional[int] = None,
        task_id: Optional[int] = None,
        parent_id: Optional[int] = None,
        attachments: Optional[List[Tuple[str, bytes]]] = None,
    ) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(author_id)
            targets = [project_id is not None, task_id is not None]
            if sum(1 for t in targets if t) != 1:
                raise ValidationError(
                    "Exactly one of project_id or task_id is required"
                )
            if project_id is not None:
                self._require_project(project_id)
            if task_id is not None:
                self._require_task(task_id)

            attachment_specs = attachments or []
            body_text = body or ""
            if not body_text.strip() and not attachment_specs:
                raise ValidationError(
                    "Message body or an attachment is required"
                )
            if parent_id is not None:
                self._require_message(parent_id)

            now = self._clock()
            built_attachments = [
                Attachment(
                    filename=filename,
                    size=len(data),
                    uploaded_by=author_id,
                    uploaded_at=now,
                )
                for filename, data in attachment_specs
            ]
            message = Message(
                id=self._next_id(),
                author_id=author_id,
                body=body_text,
                project_id=project_id,
                task_id=task_id,
                parent_id=parent_id,
                attachments=built_attachments,
                created_at=now,
                edited_at=None,
            )
            self.messages._items[message.id] = message
            return self._clone(message)

    def edit_message(
        self, message_id: int, author_id: int, new_body: str
    ) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != author_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("Only the author may edit a message")
            body_text = new_body or ""
            if not body_text.strip() and not message.attachments:
                raise ValidationError("Message body must not be empty")
            message.body = body_text
            message.edited_at = self._clock()
            return self._clone(message)

    def add_attachment(
        self, message_id: int, author_id: int, filename: str, data: bytes
    ) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != author_id:
                raise AuthorizationError(
                    "Only the author may attach to a message"
                )
            if filename is None or not filename.strip():
                raise ValidationError("filename must not be empty")
            message.attachments.append(
                Attachment(
                    filename=filename,
                    size=len(data),
                    uploaded_by=author_id,
                    uploaded_at=self._clock(),
                )
            )
            return self._clone(message)

    def list_messages_by_project(self, project_id: int) -> List[Message]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            msgs = [
                m
                for m in self.messages._items.values()
                if m.project_id == project_id  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
            ]
            return [
                self._clone(m) for m in sorted(msgs, key=lambda x: x.id)
            ]

    def list_messages_by_task(self, task_id: int) -> List[Message]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_task(task_id)
            msgs = [
                m
                for m in self.messages._items.values()
                if m.task_id == task_id  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
            ]
            return [
                self._clone(m) for m in sorted(msgs, key=lambda x: x.id)
            ]

    def delete_message(self, message_id: int, author_id: int) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != author_id:
                raise AuthorizationError(
                    "Only the author may delete a message"
                )
            del self.messages._items[message_id]

    # ==================================================================
    # 3. Performance tracking module
    # ==================================================================
    def _completed_durations(self, tasks: List[Task]) -> List[float]:
        durations = []
        for task in tasks:
            if (
                task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                and task.started_at is not None
                and task.completed_at is not None
            ):
                durations.append(
                    (task.completed_at - task.started_at).total_seconds()
                )
        return durations

    def get_completion_rate(self, user_id: int) -> float:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._tasks_for_assignee(user_id)
            if not tasks:
                return 0.0
            completed = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
            )
            return completed / len(tasks)

    def get_average_completion_time(self, user_id: int) -> Optional[float]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            durations = self._completed_durations(
                self._tasks_for_assignee(user_id)
            )
            if not durations:
                return None
            return sum(durations) / len(durations)

    def _ratings_for(self, user_id: int) -> List[Rating]:
        return [
            r
            for r in self.performance._items.values()
            if r.ratee_id == user_id  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
        ]

    def submit_rating(
        self,
        rater_id: int,
        ratee_id: int,
        score: int,
        feedback: str = "",
        task_id: Optional[int] = None,
    ) -> Rating:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(rater_id)
            self._require_user(ratee_id)
            if rater_id == ratee_id:
                raise ConflictError("Users cannot rate themselves")
            if not isinstance(score, int) or isinstance(score, bool):
                raise ValidationError("score must be an integer 1-5")
            if score < 1 or score > 5:
                raise ValidationError("score must be between 1 and 5")
            if task_id is not None:
                self._require_task(task_id)
            rating = Rating(
                id=self._next_id(),
                rater_id=rater_id,
                ratee_id=ratee_id,
                score=score,
                feedback=feedback or "",
                task_id=task_id,
                created_at=self._clock(),
            )
            self.performance._items[rating.id] = rating
            return self._clone(rating)

    def get_ratings_summary(self, user_id: int) -> Dict[str, object]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            ratings = self._ratings_for(user_id)
            count = len(ratings)
            average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / count if count else 0.0
            )
            return {
                "count": count,
                "average": average,
                "ratings": [self._clone(r) for r in sorted(
                    ratings, key=lambda x: x.id
                )],
            }

    def get_user_dashboard(self, user_id: int) -> Dict[str, object]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._tasks_for_assignee(user_id)
            total = len(tasks)
            completed = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
            )
            in_progress = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS
            )
            not_started = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED
            )
            durations = self._completed_durations(tasks)
            avg_time = sum(durations) / len(durations) if durations else None
            ratings = self._ratings_for(user_id)
            rating_count = len(ratings)
            rating_average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / rating_count
                if rating_count
                else 0.0
            )
            return {
                "user_id": user_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "completed_tasks": completed,
                "in_progress_tasks": in_progress,
                "not_started_tasks": not_started,
                "completion_rate": completed / total if total else 0.0,
                "average_completion_time": avg_time,
                "rating_count": rating_count,
                "rating_average": rating_average,
            }

    # ==================================================================
    # 3. Reporting
    # ==================================================================
    def project_progress_report(self, project_id: int) -> Dict[str, object]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            tasks = [
                t
                for t in self.tasks._items.values()
                if t.project_id == project_id  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
            ]
            total = len(tasks)
            status_counts = {status.value: 0 for status in TaskStatus}
            for task in tasks:
                status_counts[task.status.value] += 1
            completed = status_counts[TaskStatus.COMPLETED.value]
            now = self._clock()
            overdue = sum(
                1
                for t in tasks
                if t.deadline is not None
                and t.deadline < now
                and t.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED
            )
            per_assignee: Dict[int, Dict[str, int]] = {}
            for task in tasks:
                if task.assignee_id is None:
                    continue
                stats = per_assignee.setdefault(
                    task.assignee_id,
                    {
                        "total": 0,
                        "completed": 0,
                        "in_progress": 0,
                        "not_started": 0,
                    },
                )
                stats["total"] += 1
                if task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    stats["completed"] += 1
                elif task.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS:
                    stats["in_progress"] += 1
                else:
                    stats["not_started"] += 1
            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.PROJECT_PROGRESS.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "status_counts": status_counts,
                "completion_rate": completed / total if total else 0.0,
                "overdue_tasks": overdue,
                "per_assignee": per_assignee,
                "generated_at": now,
            }

    def _user_row(self, user: User, tasks: List[Task]) -> Dict[str, object]:
        total = len(tasks)
        completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
        in_progress = sum(
            1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS
        )
        not_started = sum(
            1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED
        )
        durations = self._completed_durations(tasks)
        avg_time = sum(durations) / len(durations) if durations else None
        ratings = self._ratings_for(user.id)
        rating_count = len(ratings)
        rating_average = (
            sum(r.score for r in ratings) / rating_count
            if rating_count
            else 0.0
        )
        return {
            "user_id": user.id,
            "username": user.username,
            "total_tasks": total,
            "completed_tasks": completed,
            "in_progress_tasks": in_progress,
            "not_started_tasks": not_started,
            "completion_rate": completed / total if total else 0.0,
            "average_completion_time": avg_time,
            "rating_count": rating_count,
            "rating_average": rating_average,
        }

    def team_performance_report(
        self, project_id: Optional[int] = None
    ) -> Dict[str, object]:
        with self._lock:
            if project_id is not None:
                self._require_project(project_id)
                scope_tasks = [
                    t
                    for t in self.tasks._items.values()
                    if t.project_id == project_id  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
                ]
            else:
                scope_tasks = list(self.tasks._items.values())

            assignee_ids = sorted(
                {
                    t.assignee_id
                    for t in scope_tasks
                    if t.assignee_id is not None  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
                }
            )
            rows = []
            for uid in assignee_ids:
                user = self.users._items.get(uid)
                if user is None:
                    continue
                user_tasks = [
                    t for t in scope_tasks if t.assignee_id == uid  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
                ]
                rows.append(self._user_row(user, user_tasks))  # type: ignore[arg-type]
            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "rows": rows,
                "generated_at": self._clock(),
            }

    def individual_performance_report(
        self, user_id: int
    ) -> Dict[str, object]:
        with self._lock:
            user = self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = sorted(
                self._tasks_for_assignee(user_id), key=lambda x: x.id
            )
            row = self._user_row(user, tasks)
            report = {
                "report_type": ReportType.INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "tasks": [self._clone(t) for t in tasks],
                "generated_at": self._clock(),
            }
            report.update(row)
            return report

    # ==================================================================
    # 3. Report export
    # ==================================================================
    def export_report(self, report: Dict[str, object], fmt: ReportFormat):
        if not isinstance(fmt, ReportFormat):
            raise ValidationError("fmt must be a ReportFormat")
        if not isinstance(report, dict):
            raise ValidationError("report must be a report dictionary")
        if fmt == ReportFormat.CSV:
            return self._export_csv(report)
        return self._export_pdf(report)

    def _report_table(self, report: Dict[str, object]) -> List[List[object]]:
        report_type = report.get("report_type")
        if report_type == ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value:
            header = [
                "user_id",
                "username",
                "total_tasks",
                "completed_tasks",
                "in_progress_tasks",
                "not_started_tasks",
                "completion_rate",
                "average_completion_time",
                "rating_count",
                "rating_average",
            ]
            table: List[List[object]] = [header]
            for row in report.get("rows", []):  # type: ignore[union-attr]
                table.append([row.get(key, "") for key in header])
            return table
        if report_type == ReportType.PROJECT_PROGRESS.value:
            table = [["metric", "value"]]
            table.append(["project_id", report.get("project_id", "")])
            table.append(["total_tasks", report.get("total_tasks", "")])
            status_counts = report.get("status_counts", {}) or {}
            for status_value, count in status_counts.items():  # type: ignore[union-attr]
                table.append(["status_{0}".format(status_value), count])
            table.append(
                ["completion_rate", report.get("completion_rate", "")]
            )
            table.append(["overdue_tasks", report.get("overdue_tasks", "")])
            return table
        if report_type == ReportType.INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE.value:
            table = [["metric", "value"]]
            for key in (
                "user_id",
                "username",
                "total_tasks",
                "completed_tasks",
                "in_progress_tasks",
                "not_started_tasks",
                "completion_rate",
                "average_completion_time",
                "rating_count",
                "rating_average",
            ):
                table.append([key, report.get(key, "")])
            return table
        # Generic fallback for any other dictionary shape.
        table = [["key", "value"]]
        for key, value in report.items():
            if isinstance(value, (list, dict)):
                value = str(value)
            table.append([key, value])
        return table

    def _export_csv(self, report: Dict[str, object]) -> str:
        buffer = io.StringIO()
        writer = csv.writer(buffer)
        for row in self._report_table(report):
            writer.writerow(
                ["" if cell is None else cell for cell in row]
            )
        return buffer.getvalue()

    def _export_pdf(self, report: Dict[str, object]) -> bytes:
        title = "Report: {0}".format(report.get("report_type", "unknown"))
        lines = [
            ", ".join("" if cell is None else str(cell) for cell in row)
            for row in self._report_table(report)
        ]
        return self._build_pdf(title, lines)

    @staticmethod
    def _build_pdf(title: str, lines: List[str]) -> bytes:
        def escape(text: str) -> str:
            return (
                text.replace("\\", "\\\\")
                .replace("(", "\\(")
                .replace(")", "\\)")
            )

        content_lines = ["BT", "/F1 12 Tf", "50 750 Td", "14 TL"]
        first = True
        for text in [title] + lines:
            if first:
                content_lines.append("({0}) Tj".format(escape(text)))
                first = False
            else:
                content_lines.append("T*")
                content_lines.append("({0}) Tj".format(escape(text)))
        content_lines.append("ET")
        stream = "\n".join(content_lines)

        objects = [
            "<< /Type /Catalog /Pages 2 0 R >>",
            "<< /Type /Pages /Kids [3 0 R] /Count 1 >>",
            (
                "<< /Type /Page /Parent 2 0 R /MediaBox [0 0 612 792] "
                "/Resources << /Font << /F1 5 0 R >> >> /Contents 4 0 R >>"
            ),
            "<< /Length {0} >>\nstream\n{1}\nendstream".format(
                len(stream), stream
            ),
            "<< /Type /Font /Subtype /Type1 /BaseFont /Helvetica >>",
        ]

        pdf = "%PDF-1.4\n"
        offsets = []
        for index, body in enumerate(objects, start=1):
            offsets.append(len(pdf))
            pdf += "{0} 0 obj\n{1}\nendobj\n".format(index, body)
        xref_position = len(pdf)
        pdf += "xref\n0 {0}\n".format(len(objects) + 1)
        pdf += "0000000000 65535 f \n"
        for offset in offsets:
            pdf += "{0:010d} 00000 n \n".format(offset)
        pdf += "trailer\n<< /Size {0} /Root 1 0 R >>\n".format(
            len(objects) + 1
        )
        pdf += "startxref\n{0}\n%%EOF\n".format(xref_position)
        return pdf.encode("latin-1")


__all__ = [
    "TeamCollaborationManager",
    "TaskStatus",
    "UserRole",
    "ReportFormat",
    "ReportType",
    "TeamCollaborationError",
    "ValidationError",
    "NotFoundError",
    "AuthorizationError",
    "DuplicateError",
    "ConflictError",
    "Attachment",
    "User",
    "Project",
    "Task",
    "Message",
    "Rating",
]
